title: Minimize the admission of containers with added capabilities

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with added capabilities
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with capabilities assigned beyond the default set.
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. Capabilities outside this set can be added to containers which could expose them to risks of container breakout attacks. There should be at least one policy defined which prevents containers with capabilities beyond the default set from launching. If you need to run containers with additional capabilities, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods with containers which require capabilities outwith the default set will not be permitted.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that policies are present which prevent allowedCapabilities to be set to anything other than an empty array. This audit may also require that you review the securityContext in the Pod specification yaml.
input_row.remediation: Ensure that allowedCapabilities is not present in policies for the cluster unless it is set to an empty array. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on adding capabilities to containers. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/ 2. https://www.nccgroup.trust/uk/our-research/abusing-privileged-and-unprivileged- linux-containers/
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node
input_row.impact: Pods with containers which require capabilities outwith the default set will not be permitted.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_added_capabilities_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all active Pod Security Policies (PSPs) if present (`kubectl get psp -o yaml`) and/or Pod Security Admission (PSA) configurations (via Namespace labels or AdmissionConfiguration).
- For each PSP, check the `allowedCapabilities` field:
  - Ensure `allowedCapabilities` is either not present or is set to an empty array (`[]`), which means no additional Linux capabilities can be added beyond the default set.
- For Pod Security Admission (PSA), inspect Namespace labels:
  - Look for `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce` (or `audit`/`warn`) set to `restricted` or higher, which implicitly restricts adding capabilities.
- Optionally, enumerate all `Pod` and `Deployment` specs in the cluster (`kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o yaml`, `kubectl get deployments --all-namespaces -o yaml`) and inspect the `securityContext.capabilities.add` field for any non-empty values, indicating added capabilities.
- Pass Criteria:
  - All PSPs (if used) have `allowedCapabilities` unset or set to `[]`.
  - All Namespaces enforce the `restricted` Pod Security Standard via PSA labels.
  - No Pods/Deployments have containers with `securityContext.capabilities.add` set to non-empty values unless explicitly allowed by policy.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any PSP allows additional capabilities (`allowedCapabilities` contains values).
  - Any Namespace does not enforce the `restricted` PSA level.
  - Any Pod/Deployment adds capabilities not permitted by policy.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
